refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency that ensures safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is mostly atomic, focusing on teleoperation latency compatible with safety. The phrase 'compatible with safe remote operation' is kept to emphasize safety without specifying how latency is achieved or measured, maintaining solution neutrality. No multiple obligations are present that require splitting.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the end-to-end communication latency during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify that it meets the safety criteria (e.g., p95 latency ≤ 150 ms). Conduct safety assessments to confirm that the latency does not compromise remote operation safety.

candidate_dps: Implement a communication protocol optimized for low-latency end-to-end data transfer
Design a system architecture that prioritizes real-time data processing and minimal buffering
Incorporate adaptive data compression and prioritization to reduce transmission delays
Establish a dedicated communication channel with guaranteed quality of service (QoS) parameters
Use predictive control algorithms to compensate for latency in teleoperation feedback loops
selected_dp.selected_dp: Design a system architecture that prioritizes real-time data processing and minimal buffering

potential_sources: Tight coupling between communication protocol and control algorithms
coupling_check.notes: Selected DP focuses on system architecture to minimize latency, which supports independence from specific communication protocols or control strategies, reducing coupling risk.

assumptions_risks: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters to meet latency targets
Operational policies do not introduce additional delays or constraints
Real-time processing capabilities are sufficient to handle data without introducing buffering delays
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including adaptive mechanisms to handle variable network conditions and ensure latency targets are met under all operational scenarios.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core system architecture responsible for real-time data processing and minimal buffering, integrating necessary components and managing data flows.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Bi-directional data exchange for real-time telemetry data streams.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Network communication interface for data transmission and reception.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as NGHE Solution is the core system handling real-time processing; minimal diffusion as most processing is internal.
Vehicle module is more about physical control and less about data processing; would require significant diffusion to handle real-time processing.
Teleoperation involves remote control and may handle some data, but real-time processing is broader than teleoperation scope.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety monitoring, not on general real-time data processing; high diffusion expected.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary to contain the DP of real-time data processing with minimal buffering, as it is designed to handle core system architecture and data flows, ensuring high cohesion and low diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Teleoperation if some real-time data processing is tightly coupled with remote control functions.
Interfaces with Vehicle and Safety Services may require careful coordination to avoid unintended diffusion of processing logic.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: If containment risks persist, consider refining the DP to explicitly separate real-time data processing core functions from teleoperation-specific processing to improve modular clarity without becoming implementation-specific.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically addresses the FR by focusing on a system architecture that prioritizes real-time data processing and minimal buffering, which directly supports achieving the teleoperation latency requirement (p95 ≤ 150 ms) for safe remote operation.

diffusion_risks: Potential overlap with Teleoperation module if some real-time data processing is tightly coupled with remote control functions.
Interfaces with Vehicle and Safety Services modules may cause unintended diffusion of processing logic.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion (containment score 9) and low diffusion (diffusion score 2), making it an appropriate IM boundary for the DP. Other modules have lower containment and higher diffusion scores, indicating less suitability for containing the DP.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through the IM is feasible by measuring end-to-end communication latency (p95 ≤ 150 ms) using the NGHE Solution module's telemetry and network interfaces under agreed network conditions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution IM boundary is clear and well justified, with the module encompassing core real-time data processing and minimal buffering functions necessary to meet the FR.

recommended_revisions: Consider refining the DP to explicitly separate core real-time data processing functions from teleoperation-specific processing to reduce overlap and improve modular clarity.
Carefully coordinate interfaces between NGHE Solution and Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules to minimize diffusion of processing logic and maintain architectural integrity.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed, indicating that the current DP and IM adequately address the FR with clear allocation and feasible verification. However, the recommended revisions highlight potential overlap and diffusion risks between the NGHE Solution module and other modules (Teleoperation, Vehicle, Safety Services). Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to further decompose the FR into next-level sub-FRs to explicitly separate core real-time data processing from teleoperation-specific processing. This will help reduce overlap, improve modular clarity, and better manage interface diffusion risks, thereby enhancing architectural integrity and safety assurance.
next_level_sub_frs: Real-time Data Processing with Minimal Buffering
Interface Management with Vehicle and Safety Services Modules

revise.why: To explicitly separate core real-time data processing functions from teleoperation-specific processing, reducing overlap and improving modular clarity as recommended.

assumptions: The current DP and IM sufficiently meet the top-level FR requirements.
The NGHE Solution module is the primary IM boundary for the DP with high cohesion and low diffusion.
Interfaces with other modules pose diffusion risks that can be mitigated by clearer functional separation.
open_risks: Potential overlap of processing logic between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules.
Diffusion of processing logic through interfaces with Vehicle and Safety Services modules.
evidence_needed: Verification data showing end-to-end communication latency (p95 ≤ 150 ms) under agreed network conditions.
Architectural analysis demonstrating reduced overlap and diffusion after sub-FR decomposition and DP refinement.
